title: Bart Star

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's car crashes in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated scream
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy but routine at this point in the series

jokes.text: Well, your cholesterol level is lethally high, Homer... but I'm more concerned about your gravy level.
jokes.setup: Doctor discusses Homer's high cholesterol in serious medical tone
jokes.punchline: Reveals an even more absurd concern - gravy level as medical metric
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist humor that escalates the medical consultation into pure nonsense

jokes.text: Now wait a second. You doctors have been telling us to drink eight glasses of gravy a day.
jokes.setup: Homer's confused response to medical advice
jokes.punchline: Mishears 'water' as 'gravy' - classic Homer stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - takes standard health advice and corrupts it through misunderstanding

jokes.text: [Laughing] Well, you're a little confused. Oh, confused, would we?
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert gently corrects Homer's gravy misconception
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response reveals he knows he's confused but won't admit it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's stubbornness when caught being wrong

jokes.text: You can't tell me what to do. [Tone] - [Higher-pitched Tone] - Nothing yet. - [Very High-pitched Tone] - [Both Screaming]
jokes.setup: Hearing test instruction to raise hand at tone
jokes.punchline: Homer rebelliously refuses, then both scream when tone gets painfully high
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation from defiance to physical comedy payoff

jokes.text: I'm not talking about some sort of charity rock concert.
jokes.setup: Instructor clarifies what 'first aid' means
jokes.punchline: References the common 'Aid' concerts like Live Aid
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke that's competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: [Chuckles] Serious injuries. Oh, that's gold.
jokes.setup: Instructor talks about treating serious injuries
jokes.punchline: Barney finds the concept of serious injuries hilarious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Barney - his drunk logic finds pain funny

jokes.text: Mom, Mom. I stepped in a bear trap. Sounds like you need some... first aid.
jokes.setup: Bart claims to have stepped in a bear trap
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual suggestion of first aid for extreme injury
jokes.explanation: Good understatement comedy - treating severe injury as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: I loved your last McBain movie, Mr. Wolfcastle. Quit stalling, fatty.
jokes.setup: Homer compliments the action star politely
jokes.punchline: Wolfcastle immediately insults him during workout
jokes.explanation: Nice character contrast - fan worship meets brutal trainer reality

jokes.text: Come on! Even my fat mama can touch her toes.
jokes.setup: Wolfcastle encourages Homer to stretch
jokes.punchline: Insults him by comparing to his 'fat mama'
jokes.explanation: Fairly generic fat joke elevated slightly by Wolfcastle's character

jokes.text: Hey, Bart, we wear the same underpants.
jokes.setup: Homer's pants rip during exercise
jokes.punchline: Reveals matching underwear in embarrassing moment
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassment comedy with father-son bonding element

jokes.text: Hey, tubby, want another Pop-Tart, tubby?
jokes.setup: Nelson bullying someone about weight
jokes.punchline: Repeats 'tubby' for emphasis in typical Nelson style
jokes.explanation: Standard Nelson bullying behavior - in character but unremarkable

jokes.text: I'm comfortable with who I am. You're a disgrace to this family.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy responds to fat-shaming with dignity
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicts with harsh family judgment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contrast and Homer's brutal honesty as a father

jokes.text: Yeah, but I could get seriously hurt. Then it's settled.
jokes.setup: Bart expresses reasonable concern about football injury
jokes.punchline: Homer treats this as a selling point, not a deterrent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting logic - danger becomes reason to do something

jokes.text: Team sports will keep you away from temptations like rock music and girls. [Both] Yea!
jokes.setup: Ned explains benefits of team sports to his sons
jokes.punchline: Boys enthusiastically approve of avoiding normal teenage interests
jokes.explanation: Good Flanders family dynamic - overly wholesome parenting and compliant children

jokes.text: Ah, can't I just have the surgery?
jokes.setup: Milhouse wants to avoid exercise for weight loss
jokes.punchline: Prefers surgical solution as easier option
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about childhood laziness taken to extreme medical solution

jokes.text: Sure. One helmet coming up. I was thinking more like protection for... down there. Oh. Why didn't you say so? Knee pads.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for protection for sensitive area
jokes.punchline: Clerk repeatedly misunderstands euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Classic miscommunication comedy with escalating frustration

jokes.text: Look, I want a cup. Cup. Could you spell that? C-U-P. I wanna C-U- Oh, my God! [Groans]
jokes.setup: Homer spells out 'cup' letter by letter
jokes.punchline: Clerk mishears as profanity ('see you pee')
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay payoff to the sporting goods miscommunication sequence

jokes.text: Okay, Milhouse, let's try out the new cup. [Grunts] [Chuckles] Again.
jokes.setup: Testing protective cup equipment
jokes.punchline: Bart repeatedly hits Milhouse to test protection
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Bart's sadistic streak and Milhouse as victim

jokes.text: Uh, Ralph, that's a basketball.
jokes.setup: Football practice with equipment confusion
jokes.punchline: Ralph brought wrong type of ball
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent confusion that's endearingly stupid

jokes.text: Ralph, you'll be on special teams. I'm special.
jokes.setup: Ned assigns Ralph to special teams
jokes.punchline: Ralph misunderstands as comment on his mental state
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ralph joke - perfect misunderstanding that's both innocent and darkly funny

jokes.text: [Groans] Careful, Bart. You'll break my calculator, by which I mean my head.
jokes.setup: Martin warns Bart to be careful during contact
jokes.punchline: Reveals his pretentious way of referring to his own brain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character joke - intellectual pretension meets physical vulnerability

jokes.text: After all, what civilized person would play a game with the skin of an innocent pig?
jokes.setup: Lisa objects to football on moral grounds
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of leather football
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment - righteous indignation taken to comic extreme

jokes.text: Well, actually, Lisa, these balls are synthetic. And for every ball we buy, a dollar goes to Amnesty International.
jokes.setup: Lisa's moral objection to leather footballs
jokes.punchline: Ned reveals overly progressive ball purchasing policy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Flanders character moment - overly conscientious to absurd degree

jokes.text: Who are we? [All] The Wildcats! Who are we gonna beat? The Wildcats!
jokes.setup: Standard team cheer asking who they'll beat
jokes.punchline: Kids confusedly cheer for beating themselves
jokes.explanation: Good sports comedy - kids' confusion about competitive chants

jokes.text: Hey, Flanders, you're the worst coach this team has ever had! He's the only coach this team has ever had. And the season hasn't even started yet.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizes Flanders' coaching ability
jokes.punchline: Marge points out the logical impossibility of his complaint
jokes.explanation: Great Homer/Marge dynamic - his illogical criticism met with her reasonable response

jokes.text: Yeah, well, he's... wearing that hat like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Homer struggles to find valid criticism of Flanders
jokes.punchline: Resorts to completely arbitrary hat-based insult
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stubbornness - won't back down even when clearly wrong

jokes.text: You know, Homer, it's very easy to criticize. Fun too.
jokes.setup: Marge points out that criticism is easy
jokes.punchline: Homer agrees but focuses on the enjoyment aspect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - misses moral point, focuses on personal pleasure

jokes.text: Okilly-dokilly! Let's put on our game faces.
jokes.setup: Ned tries to pump up the team
jokes.punchline: Uses his characteristic 'okilly-dokilly' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Standard Flanders catchphrase use - in character but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Give me the ball and your lunch money.
jokes.setup: Nelson demands the football during play
jokes.punchline: Also demands lunch money as if this were schoolyard bullying
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nelson joke - merges football aggression with his bullying instincts

jokes.text: Oops.
jokes.setup: Bart apparently makes a significant mistake during play
jokes.punchline: Casual 'oops' response to what was likely a major error
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to apparent football mishap

jokes.text: I've been carried out of Moe's like that hundreds of times.
jokes.setup: Player being carried off field after injury
jokes.punchline: Barney relates it to his frequent bar ejections
jokes.explanation: Good Barney character moment - alcoholism as casual reference point

jokes.text: Now I'm not the type to have a grudge for no reason. Sir, if I could just break in for a moment. [Homer] Yes, Roy. Do you have a question for Sandy Koufax? Yes. Mr. Koufax, don't you think Flanders is a big jerk?
jokes.setup: Homer calls sports radio claiming no unreasonable grudges
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to get famous athlete to validate his petty complaint
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - denies pettiness while being extremely petty

jokes.text: What? Flanders! What? What is it? Game's out there! [Laughing] Made you look.
jokes.setup: Homer distracts Flanders during game
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just childish 'made you look' prank
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish sabotage of Flanders - petty but in character

jokes.text: They're just words, Neddie. Words can't hurt you. Ow! That's it!
jokes.setup: Maude reassures Ned that words can't hurt
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets hit by thrown object, contradicting her point
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing - philosophical statement immediately contradicted by reality

jokes.text: Dad, that was really mean. I know, sweetie. And I got him right in the- Uh-oh!
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizes Homer's meanness
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly starts to brag before realizing Flanders is there
jokes.explanation: Good Homer caught-in-the-act moment - pride turns to panic

jokes.text: it seems like anyone with half a brain could coach better than you. Uh-huh? Half a brain, huh? Well, you know what? It sounds like you just volunteered.
jokes.setup: Homer insults Flanders' coaching intelligence
jokes.punchline: Flanders turns the insult into a job offer
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal - Homer's criticism backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: Then why are you running the obstacle course? Are you wearing my self-defense whistle?
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Bart's participation after saying he doesn't want to be on team
jokes.punchline: Shifting to accusation about stolen whistle
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Lisa's sudden shift to property concern

jokes.text: Wipe that smile off your face. What did I do?
jokes.setup: Homer angrily confronts Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent confusion about what he did wrong
jokes.explanation: Good coaching tyranny moment - Homer's unreasonable anger at happiness

jokes.text: Well, if you know a better way for me to live through my son, I'd like to hear it.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizes Homer's pressure on Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer openly admits his vicarious living strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line - brutally honest about living vicariously through his son

jokes.text: Just don't screw up like you always do.
jokes.setup: Young Homer about to perform gymnastics
jokes.punchline: His father's 'encouragement' is pure negativity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson parenting - destructively honest negativity

jokes.text: Ah. That's what I get for having faith in ya.
jokes.setup: Homer fails at gymnastics routine
jokes.punchline: Abe blames himself for having any expectations
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to setup - Abe's toxic parenting continues

jokes.text: Well, I'm not gonna make the same mistake. From now on, I'm gonna be kinder to my son and meaner to my dad.
jokes.setup: Homer resolves to break cycle of bad parenting
jokes.punchline: Decides to redirect cruelty rather than eliminate it
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - learns wrong lesson but in character way

jokes.text: [Thinking] It's gotta be a trick. Run like the wind.
jokes.setup: Homer chases Bart offering hugs and encouragement
jokes.punchline: Bart assumes it must be a trap due to past experience
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic - Bart's justified suspicion of Homer's kindness

jokes.text: Bart didn't get cut. What a surprise.
jokes.setup: Homer announces team cuts but keeps Bart
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic comment about obvious nepotism
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment - cuts through Homer's transparent favoritism

jokes.text: He calls me 'Coach' just like everyone else. Which he'll be doing as our new starting quarterback!
jokes.setup: Homer claims Bart gets no special treatment
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives him the most important position
jokes.explanation: Good immediate contradiction - Homer's lies exposed instantly

jokes.text: Oh, Rod, you don't have to. You're cut.
jokes.setup: Homer tells team to run laps
jokes.punchline: Casually reminds cut player he doesn't belong
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Since your mother yelled at me. Now how about that hug?
jokes.setup: Bart asks when Homer started having faith in him
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest answer about Marge's influence
jokes.explanation: Good Homer honesty - admits his parenting changes come from external pressure

jokes.text: Not so easy to keep your mouth shut now, is it, Flanders?
jokes.setup: Homer taking over coaching duties from Flanders
jokes.punchline: Taunting Flanders for being quiet after Homer forced him out
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pettiness - creating situation then mocking victim of that situation

jokes.text: Excellent ploy, Son. You panicked, but you didn't lose your cool.
jokes.setup: Bart runs in terror from opposing players
jokes.punchline: Homer spins cowardice as strategic brilliance
jokes.explanation: Good coaching delusion - Homer reframes obvious failure as success

jokes.text: Give me a 'B'? I won't give you a 'B,' but I'll tear you a new 'A.'
jokes.setup: Someone asks for cheerleading letter
jokes.punchline: Nelson threatens violence using letter-based wordplay
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nelson line - combines wordplay with his threatening personality perfectly

jokes.text: - Give me a 'B'? - I won't give you a 'B,' but I'll tear you a new 'A.'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to lead cheer for Bart
jokes.punchline: Hostile crowd member's crude wordplay threat
jokes.explanation: Clever crude wordplay using alphabet letters for anatomical threat

jokes.text: You ruined our undefeated season! You ruined everything, ruiner!
jokes.setup: Martin angrily confronts Bart about poor performance
jokes.punchline: Escalates to calling him 'ruiner' - made-up but emphatic insult
jokes.explanation: Good Martin anger - his vocabulary creates childish but emphatic insult

jokes.text: You're going to heaven.
jokes.setup: Nelson threatens Bart with death
jokes.punchline: Ralph offers innocent interpretation of dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ralph moment - innocent misunderstanding of death threat

jokes.text: Excuse me, son. Wow! Joe Namath.
jokes.setup: Random encounter with famous quarterback
jokes.punchline: Bart's starstruck recognition
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity cameo setup - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: It was just vapor lock. Remember what I told you.
jokes.setup: Joe Namath promises to give quarterback advice
jokes.punchline: Leaves before giving advice, only mentions car trouble
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - builds up advice moment then delivers car maintenance instead

jokes.text: Well, I know you don't want to disappoint Dad... but how do you feel about lying to him? Good.
jokes.setup: Lisa offering moral guidance about disappointing Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate enthusiasm for deception as alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bart's moral compass pointing toward deception

jokes.text: Oh, your mother's not pregnant, is she?
jokes.setup: Bart says he has bad news
jokes.punchline: Homer's mind jumps to pregnancy fear
jokes.explanation: Good Homer panic response - reveals his biggest domestic fear

jokes.text: Come on, guys. We can't play without Bart. He's the heart of this team. - No, I'm not. I suck. - It's true. He does.
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic speech about Bart's importance
jokes.punchline: Bart and team immediately contradicting with harsh truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Homer's delusions - even Bart agrees with harsh assessment

jokes.text: You don't get it, do you? I don't want to be your stupid quarterback! I quit!
jokes.setup: Homer continues pressuring Bart despite obvious unhappiness
jokes.punchline: Bart finally explodes with honest rejection
jokes.explanation: Good emotional payoff - Bart's frustration finally boils over

jokes.text: Well, I got news for you, mister! You can't quit! You're cut!
jokes.setup: Bart quits the team in frustration
jokes.punchline: Homer tries to save face by claiming he fired him instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer ego protection - can't let Bart quit so reframes as firing

jokes.text: Little guy's just got a mind of his own. - Mom, please! We're eating. - Well, at least I'm making conversation.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing medical condition at dinner
jokes.punchline: Marge's defense of inappropriate dinner conversation
jokes.explanation: Good family dinner awkwardness with Marge's misplaced pride in conversation

jokes.text: It must have been you. You've quit every job you've ever had- cop, pretzel vender, church counselor, professional gambler.
jokes.setup: Homer blames Marge for Bart being a quitter
jokes.punchline: Lists his own extensive quitting history while blaming her
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer hypocrisy - blames Marge while listing his own quitting pattern

jokes.text: Well, I just wanted to tell you I'm a big quitter too. And I quit.
jokes.setup: Homer calls Mr. Burns to make a point about quitting
jokes.punchline: Actually quits his job to prove the point
jokes.explanation: Good Homer impulsiveness - makes dramatic gesture without thinking through consequences

jokes.text: [Marge] Homer, Mr. Burns can't see you winking. So- [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer apparently winking during phone call
jokes.punchline: Marge points out futility, Homer realizes he quit for real
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup and Homer's delayed realization of consequences

jokes.text: Ah, I don't want to bother Mom at work.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests celebrating at Hooters
jokes.punchline: Bart implies Marge works there
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - innocent delivery of devastating implication about family finances

jokes.text: Such a product does not exist, sir. I think you must have dreamed it.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for beer with candy floating in it (Skittlebräu)
jokes.punchline: Apu's matter-of-fact dismissal of Homer's invented product
jokes.explanation: Good Apu moment - politely corrects Homer's alcohol-fueled fantasy

jokes.text: Oh. Well, then just give me a six-pack and a couple of bags of Skittles.
jokes.setup: Learning his dream product doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical workaround to create it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - if product doesn't exist, he'll make it himself with components

jokes.text: I was blinded to your stinky performance.
jokes.setup: Homer apologizes for being too encouraging
jokes.punchline: Delivers brutal honest assessment as if it's kindness
jokes.explanation: Good Homer parenting - thinks brutal honesty is an improvement

jokes.text: Okay, half of you vibrate that way, two of you fall down... Nelson, you just spin around in a circle.
jokes.setup: Homer drawing up final play strategy
jokes.punchline: His 'strategy' is completely random physical actions
jokes.explanation: Good coaching incompetence - treats football like interpretive dance

jokes.text: You know, we had a lot of fun tonight. But there's nothing funny about... vapor lock.
jokes.setup: Joe Namath delivers serious PSA-style message
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'wisdom' Bart was supposed to remember was about car maintenance
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff - the promised football wisdom was actually automotive advice

jokes.text: All of those people are cut. You're cut too, shushy!
jokes.setup: Homer reading long list of production staff cuts
jokes.punchline: Cuts someone for telling him to be quiet
jokes.explanation: Good meta ending - Homer's power trip extends to behind-the-scenes staff

tmdb_overview: When Homer becomes the coach of a peewee football team, he replaces star quarterback Nelson with the less talented Bart. Facing his teammates’ scorn, Bart looks for a way out but finds a better way to show his team spirit.
insight: Bart's football obsession generates 72 jokes in 25 minutes, just missing elite tier at 79.9.
